old_value:
{"id":"project-LICHEN-s1zp","title":"coap_client.c: timeout_ms overflow check uses UINT32_MAX/2","description":"File: lichen/subsys/lichen/coap/coap_client.c
Lines: 53 and 377-379

The timeout limit is set to prevent overflow when doubling:
```c
#define COAP_MAX_REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS (UINT32_MAX / 2U)
...
if (timeout_ms > COAP_MAX_REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS) {
    return LICHEN_COAP_ERR_INVALID_PARAM;
}
```

At line 538: `K_MSEC(timeout_ms * 2)`

This is correct for preventing overflow, but:
1. K_MSEC converts ms to ticks, which may further multiply the value
2. If timeout_ms is close to UINT32_MAX/2, timeout_ms*2 is close to UINT32_MAX
3. K_MSEC(UINT32_MAX) may overflow depending on tick rate

Should use k_timeout_t arithmetic or K_FOREVER for very large timeouts.

Category: CORRECTNESS - potential overflow","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:43:57.632205Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-12T06:43:57.632237Z"}

new_value: Fixed: updated timeout handling with k_timeout_t ternary + K_FOREVER guard in coap_client.c:282,539+ (avoids K_MSEC(*2) overflow). MAX kept /2U. Documented race. 3 codereviews passed clean after P4 fixes.
